title: Hyderabadi Biryani
description: Fragrant rice layered with marinated meat and aromatic spices. A celebratory one-pot meal.

steps.summary: Marinate meat in yogurt and spices.
subSteps: Mix yogurt with ginger-garlic paste and salt.
Add green chillies and half the cilantro-mint mixture.
Add chicken pieces and marinate for at least 2 hours.
Longer marination (4-6 hours or overnight) gives better flavor.
Bring water to boil with whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves).
Cook until 70% done (rice should be firm, not fully cooked).
steps.tip: Rice should still have a bite to it — it will fully cook in the biryani.

steps.summary: Layer rice and marinated meat and cook together.
subSteps: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot (preferably 5-liter capacity).
Layer half the marinated chicken at the bottom.
Spread half the parboiled rice over it.
Sprinkle remaining cilantro-mint, some fried onions.
Layer remaining chicken, then remaining rice.
Top with fried onions, cilantro, and mint.
Cover with aluminum foil, then place a tight-fitting lid.
Cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms.
Reduce to low heat and cook for 30 minutes (dum cooking).
Turn off heat and let it rest for 5 minutes without opening.
steps.tip: Dum cooking (steaming in a sealed pot) is key to biryani flavor and texture. Don't open the lid while cooking!

seo.metaTitle: Hyderabadi Biryani Recipe — Authentic One-Pot Meal
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make restaurant-quality Hyderabadi biryani at home with this detailed recipe guide.
